topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias stared at the screen, his coffee cup trembling slightly in his hand. The quarterly compensation report had just landed, and his eyes kept snagging on the figure next to Clara’s name. A slow, almost imperceptible tightening began in his chest, spreading outward like a cold tide. He reread the numbers, his brow furrowing deeply, trying to reconcile the data with his own understanding of the company's pay structure. When he finally looked up, Clara was gathering her files, a faint, almost imperceptible smile playing on her lips. "Everything alright, Elias?" she asked, her tone perfectly level, which only made the internal shift in him feel more pronounced. He cleared his throat, the sound surprisingly loud in the quiet office. "Just... processing the figures," he managed, his voice a little too high.

[story 2]

I leaned back in my chair, the worn leather groaning under my weight. The spreadsheet swam before my eyes, and my fingers hovered over the mouse, unable to commit to a decision. When I finally saw the discrepancy between my salary and that of the intern, Sarah, a strange lightness washed over me, quickly followed by a sudden, sharp intake of breath. My jaw went slack for a moment. I blinked several times, certain I was misreading the column. Then, I looked back down, and the numbers remained starkly clear. I let out a small, involuntary puff of air, shaking my head slightly as if trying to dislodge the sight. "Wow," I murmured, the word sounding hollow even to my own ears.

[story 3]

The fluorescent lights of the cubicle hummed with an irritating monotony. I had been meticulously reviewing the departmental budget, focusing intently on allocations, when the actual salary breakdown for the junior analyst section came into view. My gaze snagged on Clara’s entry, and the air seemed to thin out around me. A distinct clenching sensation gripped my stomach, a physical reaction I couldn't suppress. I dropped the pen I was holding; it clattered onto the desk. I swallowed hard, my throat suddenly dry, and I forced myself to focus on a distant water stain on the ceiling, trying to regain some semblance of composure.
